question: What does WURI assess and what does it stand for?
gold_answer: WURI stands for the World University Rankings for Innovation. It is a ranking system that assesses the societal and industry contributions of higher education institutions, with a focus on their innovative approaches to education, research, and society.

response.text:
CvSU started in 1906 as the Indang Intermediate School (under the American Thomasites).

It went through several name changes — Indang Farm School (1918), Indang Rural High School (1927), Don Severino National Agriculture School (1958) — before becoming a State College in 1964 via RA 3917 (Don Severino Agricultural College, DSAC).

On January 22, 1998, RA 8468 converted DSAC into Cavite State University.

CACOF (Naic) and CCAT (Rosario) joined CvSU in 2001 via CHED Memo No. 27 s. 2000.

Today CvSU has 11 campuses in Cavite, close to 100 curricular programs, and more than 25,000 students — and it celebrated its 120th founding anniversary in January 2026.

Source: https://cvsu.edu.ph/history/ CvSU is led by President Dr. Ma.

Agnes P.

Nuestro, holds AACCUP Institutional Accreditation Level III, is ISO 9001:2015 certified, ranked 217th in WURI 2026, is the DA-designated National Center for Research and Development for Coffee and Urban Agriculture, and received its 5th IPOPHL Platinum and first Palladium Award in 2026.

The main campus honors Don Severino de las Alas, the educator and philanthropist who donated the land where the school began.

(Rankings, board-exam results, and accreditation levels cited here are as of 2026 — verify the latest figures at https://cvsu.edu.ph.)

CvSU started in 1906 as the Indang Intermediate School (under the American Thomasites).

It went through several name changes — Indang Farm School (1918), Indang Rural High School (1927), Don Severino National Agriculture School (1958) — before becoming a State College in 1964 via RA 3917 (Don Severino Agricultural College, DSAC).

On January 22, 1998, RA 8468 converted DSAC into Cavite State University.

CACOF (Naic) and CCAT (Rosario) joined CvSU in 2001 via CHED Memo No. 27 s. 2000.

Today CvSU has 11 campuses in Cavite, close to 100 curricular programs, and more than 25,000 students — and it celebrated its 120th founding anniversary in January 2026.
